provenance.source_corpus: Monitoring effects of remediation on natural sediment recovery in Sydney Harbour, Nova Scotia

nodes.name: in situ remediation using solidification and stabilization (S/S)

source_spans.text: Remediation at the STP site began in 2009 and included in situ remediation using solidification and stabilization (S/S)
to reduce contaminant leaching rates into the surrounding environment

nodes.name: dredging operations required for construction of new port facilities

source_spans.text: during dredging operations required for construction of new port facilities in the inner Sydney Harbour
sedimentation rates were equivalent to 26 –128 cm year −1

nodes.name: large-scale dredging of less contaminated outer harbor sediments

source_spans.text: large-scale dredging of less contaminated outer harbor sediments which were discharged into a confined disposal area
natural recovery of sediments in Sydney Harbour
remediation activities at the STP site
TSS concentrations across all stations decreased in both shallow and deep samples since remediation began
Concentrations of Cu... decreased significantly during year 2... Mercury concentrations decreased significantly during remediation

natural recovery of sediments in Sydney Harbour, which were enhanced due to the positive impacts of large-scale dredging... natural recovery of sediments... at much lower concentrations than previously reported due to natural sediment recovery
This resulted in greater abundance and more diverse benthic communities (species richness) in Sydney Harbour
As the harbor sediments become less contaminated, they become more hospitable for benthic infauna
Increasing concentrations of benthic infauna may result in higher rates of bioturbation
thereby potentially slowing the rates of natural remediation
The technique of MNR can have profound economic, human, and ecological health benefits
benthic organisms respond positively by bioaccumulating less contaminants in their tissues

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:e84d43f51ba9
edges.original_sentence: Remediation at the STP site began in 2009 and included in situ remediation using solidification and stabilization (S/S) by mixing contaminated tar pond sediments with Portland cement, transforming it into a durable, solid, low-hydraulic conductivity matrix to reduce contaminant leaching rates into the surrounding environment

source_spans.text: Remediation at the STP site began in 2009 and included in situ remediation using solidification and stabilization (S/S) by mixing contaminated tar pond sediments with Portland cement, transforming it into a durable, solid, low-hydraulic conductivity matrix to reduce contaminant leaching rates into the surrounding environment

source_document.title: Monitoring effects of remediation on natural sediment recovery in Sydney Harbour, Nova Scotia

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:83be1787552e
edges.original_sentence: Increasing concentrations of benthic infauna may result in higher rates of bioturbation and the reworking of oxygen, nutrients, and contaminants from deeper sediments to surface sediments, thereby potentially slowing the rates of natural remediation
